bhamati-advaita.short_description: Advaita lineage descending from Maṇḍana through Vāchaspati holding the jīva as the locus of avidyā.

vivarana-advaita.short_description: Advaita lineage descending from Padmapāda through Prakāśātman holding Brahman as both āśraya and viṣaya of avidyā.

suresvara-line.short_description: The jñāna-only line from Sureśvara through Sarvajñātman that becomes the Brahman-as-locus-of-avidyā stream feeding Vivaraṇa.

proto-advaita.short_description: Pre-Śaṅkara identity-leaning currents (Gauḍapāda's ajāti-vāda; Sundara-Pāṇḍya's verses).

advaita-modern-revival.short_description: Late-modern Advaita continuity through the Maṭha-pontifical tradition into the twentieth century.

advaita-school-defining.short_description: Śaṅkara's own bhāṣya-corpus, the school's definitional core before the Bhāmatī/Vivaraṇa fork.

advaita-trans-school-synthesist.short_description: Late-medieval Advaitins (Appayya) who synthesize across schools, especially with Śaiva-Advaita.

advaita-vivarana-pedagogical.short_description: The pāṭhaśālā-curriculum stream (Dharmarāja's Vedānta-Paribhāṣā) crystallizing Advaita epistemology for teaching.

vadakalai.short_description: Northern Śrīvaiṣṇava school stemming from Vedānta Deśika; emphasizes Sanskrit śāstra and a monkey-hold (markaṭa-nyāya) view of grace.

tenkalai.short_description: Southern Śrīvaiṣṇava school stemming from Pillai Lokācārya; emphasizes Tamil hymnody and a cat-hold (mārjāra-nyāya) view of grace and total surrender.

vishishtadvaita-pancaratra-grounded.short_description: Earliest systematizing Śrīvaiṣṇava stratum (Nāthamuni) bridging Tamil Āḻvār hymnody and Sanskrit philosophy with Pāñcarātra ritual grounding.

vishishtadvaita-proto-systematic.short_description: Yāmuna's pre-Rāmānuja systematization of Śrīvaiṣṇava Vedānta and defense of Pāñcarātra.

vishishtadvaita-school-defining.short_description: Rāmānuja's own corpus that defines the school via the sapta-vidhā-anupapatti and śarīra-śarīri-bhāva.

vishishtadvaita-scholastic.short_description: Sub-commentarial scholasticism (Sudarśana Sūri's Śruta-Prakāśikā) that makes Rāmānuja's compressed dialectic readable.

tattvavada.short_description: Madhva's own school-defining position: pañca-bheda and svatantra/paratantra as the structure of reality.

dvaita-scholastic.short_description: The Jayatīrtha sub-commentary tradition that systematizes Madhva's prose.

navya-dvaita.short_description: The dialectical Dvaita of Vyāsatīrtha and Vijayīndra engaging Navya-Nyāya and Advaita at the highest formal level.

dvaita-scholastic-devotional.short_description: Rāghavendra's combination of high scholasticism with the living Mantrālaya devotional tradition.

aupadhika-bhedabheda.short_description: Bhāskara's adventitious-condition Bhedābheda: Brahman appears differentiated owing to upādhis, not by intrinsic nature.

svabhavika-bhedabheda.short_description: Nimbārka's natural-difference-and-non-difference: Brahman is intrinsically (not adventitiously) both same-as and distinct-from souls and world.

proto-bhedabheda.short_description: Pre-Śaṅkara bhedābheda current attested only through citations (Bhartṛprapañca, Āśmarathya).

bhedabheda-transitional.short_description: Yādava-Prakāśa's bhedābheda variant out of which Rāmānuja's Viśiṣṭādvaita emerged by polemical break.

bhedabheda-dvaitadvaita-scholastic.short_description: Śrīnivāsa's standard sub-commentary on Nimbārka, the Vedānta-Kaustubha, that fixes how the school is read in tradition.

gaudiya-school-founding.short_description: Caitanya's school-founding presence, propagated more by example and disciples than by his own treatise-writing.

gaudiya-rasa-theology.short_description: Rūpa Gosvāmī's systematic application of rasa-theory to bhakti, codifying Gauḍīya devotional aesthetics.

gaudiya.short_description: The Gauḍīya stream (Sanātana, Viśvanātha) consolidating practice, hermeneutics, and devotional manuals.

gaudiya-systematic-philosophy.short_description: Jīva Gosvāmī's Ṣaṭ-Sandarbha gives the Gauḍīya school its systematic philosophical exposition.

gaudiya-formal-vedantic.short_description: Baladeva Vidyābhūṣaṇa's Govinda-Bhāṣya formally seats the Caitanya tradition among the bhāṣyakāras.

gaudiya-modern.short_description: Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura's late-colonial Vedāntic reformulation of the Gauḍīya tradition.

shuddhadvaita-school-defining.short_description: Vallabha's pure non-dualism without māyā, defending Brahman-world identity through avikṛta-pariṇāma.

pushti-marga.short_description: The path of grace: Vallabha's living tradition consolidated by Viṭṭhalanātha as a sectarian devotional community.

pushti-marga-scholastic.short_description: Puruṣottama's standard scholastic apparatus on Vallabha's Aṇu-Bhāṣya, the form Puṣṭimārga is studied in today.

avibhaga-advaita.short_description: Vijñānabhikṣu's harmonizing Vedānta + Sāṃkhya + Yoga thesis: Brahman and world are inseparable (avibhāga) yet not numerically identical.

proto-vedanta-sutra-author.short_description: Bādarāyaṇa, the (multi-layered) author of the Brahma-Sūtra, the spine that every later school must commentate.

proto-vedanta-citation-only.short_description: Pre-Bādarāyaṇa voices known only by name within the Brahma-Sūtra (Auḍulomi, Āśmarathya, Kāśakṛtsna).

proto-vedanta-claimed-by-vishishtadvaita.short_description: Lost-corpus figure (Bodhāyana the Vṛttikāra) whose authority the Śrīvaiṣṇava school claims as restored by Rāmānuja.

proto-vedanta-claimed-by-both.display_name: Claimed by both Advaita and Viśiṣṭādvaita
proto-vedanta-claimed-by-both.short_description: Upavarṣa, cited respectfully by both Śaṅkara and Rāmānuja, contested between the schools.

proto-vedanta-bhavana-jnana-hybrid.short_description: Brahmadatta, a pre-Śaṅkara teacher of knowledge-as-bhāvanā cited by Sureśvara and Vāchaspati.

proto-advaita-asmarathya-leaning.short_description: Āśmarathya's apparent proto-bhedābheda position cited approvingly by Bādarāyaṇa.

proto-advaita-kasakrtsna-leaning.short_description: Kāśakṛtsna's apparent proto-identity-thesis cited at Brahma-Sūtra 1.4.22.

trika-recognition-school-founding.short_description: Utpaladeva's Īśvara-Pratyabhijñā-Kārikā: liberation as recognition of the always-already-Śiva nature of awareness.

trika-school-consummating.short_description: Abhinavagupta's towering synthesis of Trika tantra, recognition philosophy, and rasa-theology.

trika-school-summarizing.short_description: Kṣemarāja's compact, accessible summary (Pratyabhijñā-Hṛdayam) of Pratyabhijñā doctrine.

shabda-advaita.short_description: Bhartṛhari's grammarian thesis that Brahman is essentially śabda; influences Maṇḍana but is not Vedāntic proper.
